audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim Moab-covenant renewal and hidden-things discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Moab covenant beyond Horeb, wilderness provision, inclusive assembly, present and absent generations, anti-idolatry warning, collective catastrophe rhetoric, exile imagery, and hidden/revealed things.

verses.restored: These are words of covenant that YHWH commanded Moses to cut with children of Israel in land of Moab, besides covenant he cut with them at Horeb.
Moses called to all Israel and said to them: You saw all YHWH did before your eyes in land of Egypt to Pharaoh, all his servants, and all his land,
the great trials your eyes saw, signs, and those great wonders.
Yet YHWH has not given you a heart to know, eyes to see, or ears to hear until this day.
I led you forty years in wilderness; your garments did not wear out upon you, and your sandal did not wear out upon your foot.
Bread you did not eat, and wine or strong drink you did not drink, so you might know that I am YHWH your Divine.
You came to this place, and Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan came out to meet us for battle, and we struck them.
We took their land and gave it as inheritance to Reubenites, Gadites, and half tribe of Manasseh.
You shall keep words of this covenant and do them, so you may act wisely/prosper in all you do.
You are standing today, all of you, before YHWH your Divine: heads of your tribes, your elders, your officers, every man of Israel,
your little ones, your wives, and your sojourner who is in midst of your camp, from cutter of your wood to drawer of your water,
for you to pass into covenant of YHWH your Divine and into his oath that YHWH your Divine cuts with you today,
in order to establish you today as a people for himself and that he may be Divine for you, as he spoke to you and as he swore to your fathers, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
Not with you alone do I cut this covenant and this oath,
but with the one who stands here with us today before YHWH our Divine and with the one who is not here with us today.
For you know how we dwelt in land of Egypt and how we passed through midst of nations through which you passed.
You saw their detestable things and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold, which were with them.
Lest there be among you man or woman or clan or tribe whose heart turns today from YHWH our Divine to go serve gods of those nations, lest there be among you a root bearing poison and wormwood.
It shall be, when he hears words of this oath and blesses himself in his heart, saying, Peace shall be for me, though I walk in stubbornness of my heart, to sweep away the watered with the dry,

YHWH will not be willing to forgive him; then anger of YHWH and his jealousy will smoke against that man, and all oath written in this scroll will rest upon him, and YHWH will blot out his name from under heavens.
YHWH will separate him for evil from all tribes of Israel, according to all oaths of covenant written in this scroll of Torah/instruction.
The later generation, your children who rise after you, and foreigner who comes from distant land, shall say when they see plagues of that land and its sicknesses with which YHWH afflicted it—
all its land brimstone, salt, and burning, not sown and not sprouting and no grass rising in it, like overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which YHWH overthrew in his anger and fury—
all nations shall say, Why did YHWH do thus to this land? What is heat of this great anger?
Then they shall say, Because they abandoned covenant of YHWH, Divine of their fathers, which he cut with them when he brought them out from land of Egypt,
and they went and served other gods and bowed to them, gods they had not known and he had not apportioned to them.
Anger of YHWH burned against that land, to bring upon it all curse written in this scroll.
YHWH uprooted them from their ground in anger, fury, and great wrath and cast them into another land, as this day.
Hidden things belong to YHWH our Divine, but revealed things belong to us and to our children forever, to do all words of this Torah/instruction.

translation_notes: English and Hebrew traditions may attach verse 1 to the previous chapter; this file follows common English chapter numbering.
The covenant assembly explicitly includes leaders, men, women, children, immigrants, and laborers from woodcutter to water drawer.
The covenant reaches those present and those 'not here,' extending responsibility across generations without erasing personal agency.
The phrase about a root bearing poison warns against hidden covenant corruption and must not become permission to scapegoat minority people or families.
Stiff-necked or stubborn-heart language is internal covenant critique and must never support antisemitic stereotypes.
The devastated-land imagery is ancient covenant-curse rhetoric and cannot be used to blame modern victims of war, drought, contamination, exile, or ecological disaster.
The final verse limits speculative certainty: hidden matters belong to YHWH, while revealed instruction is given for communal practice.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 29 to justify collective punishment, antisemitism, conspiracy claims about hidden evil, religious exclusion, exile theology, or disaster blame. Center the covenant's radically broad assembly, intergenerational responsibility, humility about hidden things, and practical obedience to what has actually been revealed.
